records.ety: [OE. nou, nu, AS. nū, nu; akin to D., OS., & OHG. nu, G. nu, nun, Icel., nū, Dan., Sw., & Goth. nu, L. nunc, Gr. ny`, ny^n, Skr. nu, nū. √193. Cf. New.]

defs: At the present time; at this moment; at the time of speaking; instantly; as, I will write now.
quotes: I have a patient now living, at an advanced age, who discharged blood from his lungs thirty years ago.
They that but now, for honor and for plate, Made the sea blush with blood, resign their hate.

defs: At a time contemporaneous with something spoken of or contemplated; at a particular time referred to.
quotes: The ship was now in the midst of the sea.

defs: In present circumstances; things being as they are; -- hence, used as a connective particle, to introduce an inference or an explanation.
quotes: How shall any man distinguish now betwixt a parasite and a man of honor?
Why should he live, now nature bankrupt is?
Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now, Barabbas was a robber.
The other great and undoing mischief which befalls men is, by their being misrepresented. Now, by calling evil good, a man is misrepresented to others in the way of slander.

defs: Existing at the present time; present.
The present time or moment; the present.
quotes: Nothing is there to come, and nothing past; But an eternal now does ever last.
